Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. William Blake, Remote by the Sea 24 upvotes, 0 comments
  2. Roulette Computers: Hidden Devices That Predict Spins 18 upvotes, 0 comments
  3. Cherri – programming language that compiles to an Apple Shortuct 168 upvotes, 33 comments
  4. The ladder is missing rungs – Engineering Progression When AI Ate the Middle 48 upvotes, 7 comments
  5. The curious case of retro demo scene graphics 330 upvotes, 84 comments
  6. An NSFW filter for Marginalia search 65 upvotes, 13 comments
  7. Proactively Parasocial 24 upvotes, 3 comments
  8. Seeing Like a Spreadsheet 31 upvotes, 5 comments
  9. Do your own writing 176 upvotes, 52 comments
  10. Fedware: Government apps that spy harder than the apps they ban 198 upvotes, 61 comments
  11. How to turn anything into a router 487 upvotes, 185 comments
  12. America Is Now a Rogue Superpower 16 upvotes, 2 comments
  13. New Washington state law bans noncompete agreements 259 upvotes, 102 comments
  14. I use Excalidraw to manage my diagrams for my blog 248 upvotes, 99 comments
  15. Mathematical methods and human thought in the age of AI 177 upvotes, 70 comments
  16. Show HN: Coasts – Containerized Hosts for Agents 38 upvotes, 11 comments
  17. Build123d: A Python CAD programming library 94 upvotes, 38 comments
  18. Take better notes, by hand 140 upvotes, 63 comments
  19. Recover Apple Keychain 23 upvotes, 6 comments
  20. FTC action against Match and OkCupid for deceiving users, sharing personal data 195 upvotes, 99 comments
  21. Turning a MacBook into a touchscreen with $1 of hardware (2018) 61 upvotes, 25 comments
  22. A sea of sparks: Seeing radioactivity 34 upvotes, 13 comments
  23. CodingFont: A game to help you pick a coding font 236 upvotes, 139 comments
  24. Bird brains (2023) 266 upvotes, 171 comments
  25. OCR for construction documents does not work, we fixed it 88 upvotes, 49 comments
  26. Fibonacci's Composed Fractions 10 upvotes, 2 comments
  27. Vulnerability research is cooked 51 upvotes, 30 comments
  28. I am definitely missing the pre-AI writing era 201 upvotes, 171 comments
  29. In math, rigor is vital, but are digitized proofs taking it too far? 78 upvotes, 61 comments
  30. From Proxmox to FreeBSD and Sylve in our office lab 58 upvotes, 49 comments

Last generated 5:00am, 31 March 2026. Source here.
